candidates.basis: MeshGraphNet-style graph predictions should be equivariant to a pure node-row permutation when edge indices and node features are permuted consistently.
candidates.decision_reason: Pure graph-row relabeling is an admissible exact representation relation once production graph tensors are available.

execution_gate.reason: No raw production outputs or metric ledgers exist yet; Pure graph-row relabeling is an admissible exact representation relation once production graph tensors are available.

candidates.metric: relative L2 between source output and inverse-mapped follow-up output
candidates.output_mapping: Inverse-permute follow-up node predictions to source node order.
preconditions: Permutation preserves node and edge attributes

candidates.basis: Cylinder/vortex-shedding geometry may appear mirror-like, but inflow, boundary encoding, rollout state, and trained preprocessing must be audited before exact mirror-y is valid.
candidates.decision_reason: Treat as OOD stress until boundary/preprocessing symmetry and numerical floor are verified from official artifacts.

execution_gate.reason: No raw production outputs or metric ledgers exist yet; Treat as OOD stress until boundary/preprocessing symmetry and numerical floor are verified from official artifacts.

candidates.metric: mapped-field relative L2 over velocity/pressure channels
candidates.output_mapping: Reflect coordinates and flip transverse velocity components before comparing mapped fields.
preconditions: Geometry and boundary conditions are mirror symmetric
Training normalization does not encode an orientation

candidates.basis: A learned transient surrogate can be checked against reference-relative divergence/flux drift, but absolute conservation is not implied by a black-box checkpoint.
candidates.decision_reason: Diagnostic is plausible, but tolerance/floor and reference mapping require production raw outputs.

execution_gate.reason: No raw production outputs or metric ledgers exist yet; Diagnostic is plausible, but tolerance/floor and reference mapping require production raw outputs.

candidates.metric: relative change in divergence/flux diagnostic versus reference/source baseline
candidates.output_mapping: Compute source and follow-up divergence/flux diagnostics in the same physical coordinates.
preconditions: Mesh cells/areas or graph stencils are accessible
Reference or source-step baseline is available

candidates.basis: Perturbations that alter inlet, wall, or obstacle boundary semantics are not exact MRs for a trained vortex-shedding surrogate.
candidates.decision_reason: Boundary-changing transforms violate the SUT input contract and must not be executed as exact MRs.

execution_gate.reason: No raw production outputs or metric ledgers exist yet; Boundary-changing transforms violate the SUT input contract and must not be executed as exact MRs.

candidates.output_mapping: No output mapping unless the transformed input is contract-preserving.
preconditions: Perturbation preserves boundary labels and physical units

candidates.basis: Estimate relation-specific floating-point/operator floor from repeated production inference before assigning tolerances.
candidates.decision_reason: Numerical floor cannot be calibrated before official runtime and raw repeated outputs exist.

execution_gate.reason: No raw production outputs or metric ledgers exist yet; Numerical floor cannot be calibrated before official runtime and raw repeated outputs exist.

candidates.metric: repeat-run relative L2 / max absolute difference by output channel
candidates.output_mapping: Compare repeated source-output tensors or surface fields in the model's native output schema.
preconditions: At least two repeated source inferences can be saved as raw outputs

honesty_boundary: This is a candidate ledger only. No production MR has been executed; Task 3--5 workflow results remain blocked until official runtime/checkpoints/data/API plus raw outputs and metric ledgers exist.
